orchestrationPlan.platform: Nexus Agent Platform — Agent Orchestration Runtime Engine

modules.name: Project Foundation
modules.description: Directory structure, tooling config, workspace setup

modules.name: Strict Type System
modules.description: AgentInput, AgentOutput, AgentContext, GraphTypes, Zod validation, branded types
qualityGates: G-M5-1: tsc --noEmit with zero errors
G-M5-2: All Zod schemas pass robust test cases

modules.name: Agent Registry
modules.description: Loads exactly 18 agents from .opencode/agents/, no extension API
qualityGates: G-M3-1: getAll().length === 18
G-M3-2: All frontmatter parses without errors
G-M3-3: Unknown agent IDs return undefined

modules.name: Supabase Layer
modules.description: Repository pattern, service layer, migrations, RLS
qualityGates: G-M6-1: All 5 migrations apply/revert cleanly
G-M6-2: Each repository passes CRUD tests
G-M6-3: Repository types match M5 type expectations

modules.name: Agent Execution Engine
qualityGates: G-M1-1: Single runner executes any registered agent
G-M1-2: 3-agent chain passes context correctly
G-M1-3: DAG fan-out-fan-in completes with parallel branches
G-M1-4: Cycle detection rejects invalid DAGs

modules.name: Core Orchestration Layer
qualityGates: G-M2-1: Pipeline state machine transitions correctly
G-M2-2: Conditional routing follows correct branch
G-M2-3: Error isolation in parallel branches
G-M2-4: Context propagation through pipeline

modules.name: Externalized Prompt System
modules.description: Versioned prompts, template compilation, agent binding, hot-reload
qualityGates: G-M4-1: All 18 agents have >= 1 prompt version
G-M4-2: Version pinning resolves consistently
G-M4-3: Templates compile with real context

modules.name: n8n Integration
modules.description: Webhook-triggered, zero business logic in n8n
qualityGates: G-M7-1: Webhook endpoint returns 200 with valid payload
G-M7-2: Webhook triggers M2 pipeline execution
G-M7-3: Invalid HMAC signatures rejected with 401

modules.name: UI Dashboard
modules.description: Admin dashboard, pipeline builder, execution history
qualityGates: G-P8-1: UI displays all 18 agents from M3
G-P8-2: Pipeline builder creates valid pipeline config

modules.name: Voice Layer
qualityGates: G-M8-1: Voice compiles, enabled === false, no active connections

modules.name: Integration & QA
modules.description: Cross-module integration, full smoke test, performance validation
qualityGates: G-INT-1: End-to-end smoke test passes
G-INT-2: Performance benchmarks met
G-INT-3: Zero regression across all modules

modules.name: Documentation & Go-to-Market
modules.description: Technical docs, API reference, onboarding guide, launch assets

specContributors.Product Manager: PRDs, acceptance criteria, prioritization for all modules
specContributors.Workflow Architect: DAG specs, handoff contracts, error recovery, routing workflows
specContributors.Trend Researcher: Back-compat requirements, ecosystem analysis, tech risk assessment
specContributors.Pipeline Analyst: Pipeline health metrics, KPI schema, dashboard requirements
specContributors.Deal Strategist: Conditional routing business rules for pipeline branching
specContributors.Proposal Strategist: Output template schema, report formatting specifications
specContributors.Sales Outreach: Agent interaction UX specs, user stories for pipeline creation UI
specContributors.Customer Service: Error message tone/branding, user-facing text specifications
specContributors.Support Responder: Failure recovery workflow specs, escalation procedure definitions
specContributors.Recruitment Specialist: Agent capability assessment framework, skill matching schema
specContributors.Growth Hacker: A/B pipeline routing specs, experimental pipeline patterns
specContributors.SEO Specialist: URL structure spec, doc site meta, schema markup

metaOrchestration.role: META — operates one level above all builder agents
responsibilities: Spawn correct builder agent at correct phase
Run Dev-QA loop on each module deliverable
Enforce quality gates before phase advancement
Manage retry logic (max 3 attempts per task)
Coordinate agent handoffs with full context
metaOrchestration.runtimeRole: Agent #1 in registry — pipeline manager that other pipelines can invoke

criticalGates: G-M1-1: Single runner executes all agents
validationArtifacts: QA Report with PASS/FAIL per test case

riskRegister.mitigation: Parallelize M6 (Infrastructure Maintainer) and spec work while Backend Architect builds M5 → M3 → M1

riskRegister.description: M5 type changes after downstream modules start
riskRegister.mitigation: Interface-first contract. M5 delivers .types.ts FIRST. M1/M2 build against types not implementations.

riskRegister.description: Supabase migrations conflict with M5 types
riskRegister.mitigation: M6 types.ts imports from M5; schema changes require M5 review
Strict webhook contract in .types.ts. No business logic in n8n is enforced.
Stubs only, behind feature flag. Voice expansion is separate initiative.
Unit test asserts exact count. Any addition requires explicit spec change.
Distinct contexts documented in meta-orchestration section

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M5 types compilable and importable by all consumer modules
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: P10 Integration Test — import all types across all modules

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M3 loads exactly 18 agents, rejects unknown IDs
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: M3 unit test with exact count assertion

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M1 returns valid AgentResult for any registered agent
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: M1 integration test executing each of 18 agents

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M2 routes context correctly through multi-agent pipeline
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: M2 E2E test with 3+ agents and context assertion

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M4 prompts resolve with correct version for agent
M6 repositories return typed results matching M5 types
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: M6 unit test comparing repository output to M5 schemas

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M7 webhook triggers M2 pipeline with correct payload
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: M7 integration test calling webhook and observing M2 execution

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: M8 stubs compile but never activate without feature flag
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: P10 gate check: enable flag false, assert no active connections

criticalIntegrationPoints.description: P8 UI reads from M3 registry and M6 Supabase simultaneously
criticalIntegrationPoints.validatedBy: P8 E2E test rendering dashboard with live data

orchestrationPlan.orchestratorCommand: Please spawn an agents-orchestrator to execute complete development pipeline for project-specs/nexus-orchestration-plan.md. Run autonomous workflow: (P0 foundation) → [Backend Architect builds M5 → M3 → M1 → M2 → M4] parallel-with [Infrastructure Maintainer builds M6 → M7] → [UI Designer builds P8] → [Integration QA by TestingRealityChecker]. Each task must pass QA before advancing. Max 3 retries per task.
